headline: Intel Dips 5%, AMD Down 3%: Rising Competition and Sector Pressure Test Two of Wall Street’s Favorite Chip Stocks
summary: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) stock is down 5% in Friday trading, pulling back to around $44 after a strong run that has seen the stock nearly double over the past year. Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) stock is also in the red, slipping 3% to less than $200. Both moves reflect a broader semiconductor sector under pressure from ... headline: 2 Semiconductor Stocks to Consider Right Now and 1 That Underwhelm
summary: Semiconductors are the core infrastructure powering the Information Age. Compute-intensive AI workloads are also priming them for the next wave of secular growth, so it’s no wonder the industry has outperformed the market over the past six months, delivering returns of 34.6% while the S&P 500 was flat.

headline: The Nasdaq Sinks As Super Micro AI Chip Smuggling to China Takes Center Stage | SMCI IXIC

headline: Meta Joins OCI Group As AI Links And Valuation Draw Investor Focus
summary: Meta Platforms joined AMD, Broadcom, Microsoft, NVIDIA and OpenAI to form the Optical Compute Interconnect (OCI) MSA, an industry group focused on open optical links for AI infrastructure. The OCI MSA aims to create an open, multi vendor specification for high performance, power efficient optical interconnects used in large scale AI clusters. This move positions Meta as an active participant in setting technical standards that could influence how future AI data centers are built and...

headline: Ecolab to buy CoolIT for $4.75 billion to tap into AI data center boom
summary: Ecolab said on Friday it would acquire CoolIT Systems from KKR for about $4.75 billion ‌in cash, as the water solutions firm seeks ‌to capitalize on surging demand for liquid cooling in artificial intelligence-driven data ​centers.  Owned by funds managed by KKR, CoolIT ⁠designs and manufactures liquid cooling systems used by hyperscale and colocation operators.  Ecolab expects CoolIT’s hardware ​and thermal engineering to ‌complement its own strengths in water, chemistry and digital monitoring, helping it turn into a more complete provider of cooling and fluid management.

raw.headline: Ecolab to buy CoolIT for $4.75 billion to tap into AI data center boom

headline: Why Celestica (TSX:CLS) Is Up 8.2% After Teaming With AMD On Helios AI Networking Switches
summary: In March 2026, AMD announced a collaboration with Celestica to develop and manufacture scale-up networking switches for the Helios rack-scale AI platform, using Open Compute Project, Open-Rack-Wide designs and Ultra Accelerator Link over Ethernet to connect next-generation AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs across large AI clusters. This partnership highlights how Celestica is moving further up the AI infrastructure stack by combining its design and manufacturing strengths with AMD’s compute...
